signals.headline: Bitcoin Surges Past $75,000 on Institutional Demand | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Surges Past $75,000 on Institutional Demand | YES | HIGH confidence**

By mid-April 2026, Bitcoin shattered key resistance levels, surging past $75,000 driven by record-breaking spot Bitcoin ETF inflows, corporate treasury accumulation (MicroStrategy alone holding over 300,000 BTC), and improved regulatory clarity[4]. As of April 17, Bitcoin was trading at $75,746.90[5].

signals.headline: Kanye West Tweet by April 30
signals.raw:
Kanye West Tweet by April 30? Active with Niche Buzz | NO strong edge | LOW confidence**  
138 active Tweet markets include "Will Kanye tweet again by April 30?", trading at implied probabilities amid $27.3M volume, but no mispricing calls, whale moves, or X posts highlighted in real-time data[3]. No confirmed announcements resolving broader markets noted.

signals.headline: Ethereum Follows Bitcoin Rally Higher | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Follows Bitcoin Rally Higher | YES | HIGH confidence**

Ethereum surged over 50% in early April following an Iran ceasefire announcement, significantly outperforming Bitcoin's 8% gain over the same window[3]. Ethereum was trading around $2,358.26 on April 17[5] and $2,317.80 on April 20[6], reflecting higher volatility and stronger correlation with risk-on sentiment compared to Bitcoin[3]
